steps.title: Fastest path: track what changes
steps.content: When the same amount is added to every value, the mean and median move by that amount. The range and standard deviation stay the same because the spacing between values does not change.

steps.title: Set up the structure of the data set
steps.content: With 27 numbers and a mean of 33, the total sum is $27 \times 33 = 891$. Since 27 is odd, the median is the 14th value when sorted. That means 13 values are less than 33, one value equals 33 (the median itself), and 13 values are greater than 33.

steps.title: Compute the effect on the sum and mean
steps.content:
The transformation adds 7 to each of the 13 values above the median and subtracts 7 from each of the 13 values below the median. Net change to sum: $+7 \times 13 - 7 \times 13 = 0$. The sum remains 891 and the mean remains $891 \div 27 = 33$.
  • The mean remains 33 in both sets, so it is not the answer.
  • The sum remains 891 in both sets, so it is not the answer.

steps.title: Check whether the median changes
steps.content:
Subtracting 7 from any value that was less than 33 keeps it less than 33; adding 7 to any value greater than 33 keeps it greater than 33. The relative order is preserved, so the 14th value remains 33.
  • The median remains 33, so it is not the answer.
